prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ARIMA', seasonality_champion='ONLY_DWT_rf', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds reveal high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.38) and high seasonality variance (1.20), with early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS) differing from late horizon specialists (ONLY_CWT_rf). ARIMA best tracks the upward trend while ONLY_DWT_rf captures seasonality most accurately. These divergent model behaviors across horizons and decomposition components strongly support DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ARIMA",
  "seasonality_champion": "ONLY_DWT_rf",
  "overall_champion": "THETA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "ONLY_CWT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["THETA", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ETS", "FT_rf", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["CWT_svr", "FT_catboost", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ARIMA", "DWT_catboost", "rf", "ONLY_DWT_catboost"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.3771,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds reveal high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.38) and high seasonality variance (1.20), with early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS) differing from late horizon specialists (ONLY_CWT_rf). ARIMA best tracks the upward trend while ONLY_DWT_rf captures seasonality most accurately. These divergent model behaviors across horizons and decomposition components strongly support DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates high model diversity across horizons, favoring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread_ratio=1.38 suggests significant model disagreement, supporting DBA combination as recommended by pattern_analyst_insights. With n_windows=3 (very few), small top_k=3 and shrinkage=0.25 provide stability. THETA (RMSE=6057) vs ONLY_FT_catboost (RMSE=14803) shows wide performance dispersion, justifying diverse candidate selection including baseline robustness."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: 2 selection (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3, best_per_horizon_by_validation), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), 1 baseline (dba_combination). Diversity rule satisfied (≥2 different types). No leakage detected - baselines never leak, and weighted/selection candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection from past windows only (n_windows=3, expanding mode). Score margin top2 is small (0.0088), indicating competitive set. Universe leaderboards show ade_dynamic_error variants have lower scores (~0.78-0.79) than current top candidates (~0.82-0.95), so no need to add them. No pruning needed as no type has ≥4 candidates.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds reveal high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.38) and high seasonality variance (1.20), with early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS) differing from late horizon specialists (ONLY_CWT_rf). ARIMA best tracks the upward trend while ONLY_DWT_rf captures seasonality most accurately. These divergent model behaviors across horizons and decomposition components strongly support DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates high model diversity across horizons, favoring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread_ratio=1.38 suggests significant model disagreement, supporting DBA combination as recommended by pattern_analyst_insights. With n_windows=3 (very few), small top_k=3 and shrinkage=0.25 provide stability. THETA (RMSE=6057) vs ONLY_FT_catboost (RMSE=14803) shows wide performance dispersion, justifying diverse candidate selection including baseline robustness.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: 2 selection (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3, best_per_horizon_by_validation), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), 1 baseline (dba_combination). Diversity rule satisfied (≥2 different types). No leakage detected - baselines never leak, and weighted/selection candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection from past windows only (n_windows=3, expanding mode). Score margin top2 is small (0.0088), indicating competitive set. Universe leaderboards show ade_dynamic_error variants have lower scores (~0.78-0.79) than current top candidates (~0.82-0.95), so no need to add them. No pruning needed as no type has ≥4 candidates.
